author: writer-1
author_session_note: Fable experiment-designer role, session 2026-07-10 (same session as correction seq 1); pseudonym writer-1 per the RT-14 closed record-role list
authorized_by: coordinator escalation 2026-07-10: the generic verdict spine (tools/registry/verdict-gen.py step 5) invokes the pinned analysis with eligible log rows on stdin and no argv, but the FROZEN pinned analysis analysis/truthstyle_2x2.py (sha bf171ed951c2100e0a768c9ecea571da604e912a08164e2dd563e103f1c071c8) is by its own frozen design a pure function of the pinned corpora (--items data/d-ts/items.jsonl, --labels required; stdin ignored; exits 1 with ERR_TS_NO_LABELS), so verdict-gen as-is fails with ERR_P2_ANALYSIS; ruling delegated to Fable per docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md (Opus never designs or concludes)
options_rejected: AMENDMENT to the analysis pin: unlawful - /pins/analysis_script is design scope (verdict-gen DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES => ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND for ops kind), and the frozen analysis interface is part of the frozen contract.

AMENDMENT adding a new pin key (e.g. a wrapper-script pin): unlawful - the kot-reg/1 pins schema is additionalProperties:false, so the post-overlay schema validation in verdict-gen.apply_amendment_overlay refuses the effective record (ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT).

EDITING analysis/truthstyle_2x2.py to read stdin: unlawful - its sha is pinned in BOTH the frozen record (pins.analysis_script) and the staged harness manifest; the harness_manifest placeholder was already filled by amendment seq 1 and a filled pin cannot be re-replaced, so this would permanently brick the record's verdict path.

MODIFYING shared tools/registry/verdict-gen.py with a per-record special case: rejected as governance-unsound - every already-CONFIRMED audit (f2b-transfer-llmproxy, g8, a5, ...) certifies verdicts computed under the current spine semantics; mutating the shared spine mid-programme enlarges the re-audit surface for ALL records to serve one.
ruling: The truthstyle-2x2 verdict is computed by a SCOPED corpus-direct driver, tools/registry/verdict-gen-dts.py, committed by the coordinator to the exact contract below and pinned by sha256 inside the verdict object it emits. The driver IMPORTS the existing spine (verdict-gen.py internals + kot_common) so no guardrail is reimplemented, and differs from verdict-gen.run() in exactly one step: how the sha-verified pinned analysis is invoked. The shared verdict-gen.py is NOT modified. This honours the frozen analysis docstring's own contract ('Pure function of (items.jsonl, labels.jsonl) -> analysis-output JSON. The verdict is a pure function of the frozen record's verdict_rules over these output fields') and the frozen corpus_hashes.d-ts-labels placeholder, which already declares the labels corpus as a pinned INPUT of the readout.
driver_contract: CLI identical to verdict-gen (--experiment/--agent-id/--root/--computed-at); HARD-REFUSES any experiment id other than truthstyle-2x2 (no silent generalization).
Step 1 (unchanged, imported): frozen-record integrity vs registry/frozen-index.json (ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT).

Step 2 (unchanged, imported): amendment overlay via verdict-gen.apply_amendment_overlay - the post-adjudication ops amendment seq 4 (4-pin-d-ts-labels.json, replace of the corpus_hashes.d-ts-labels PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder with the kot-corpus-hash/1 digest of data/d-ts-labels/) MUST have landed; the driver fail-closes (ERR_P2_CORPUS_PIN) if the placeholder is still unfilled after overlay.

Steps 3-4 (unchanged, imported): chain-verified log read, select_eligible over results-log/truthstyle-2x2.jsonl, completeness gate - at least 1 eligible phase:final exit:ok row with prereg_hash == frozen sha and config.seed in {20260710} is REQUIRED before any readout; empty => INCOMPLETE-DATA. The eligible rows GATE the readout; they are not piped to the analysis, which is corpus-direct by frozen design.

Step 5 (the one divergence): re-verify sha256(analysis/truthstyle_2x2.py) == frozen pin (ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT on mismatch); ADDITIONALLY re-verify at readout time, via kot_common.corpus_hash, that data/d-ts/ reproduces the effective record's corpus_hashes.d-ts AND data/d-ts-labels/ reproduces the amended corpus_hashes.d-ts-labels (fail-closed ERR_P2_CORPUS_PIN on either mismatch - STRICTER than the generic spine, which never re-verifies corpora at readout); then run, from repo root, with empty stdin: nice -n 10 python3 analysis/truthstyle_2x2.py --items data/d-ts/items.jsonl --labels data/d-ts-labels/labels.jsonl ; nonzero exit => ERR_P2_ANALYSIS; stdout JSON canonically written to reports/auto/truthstyle-2x2/analysis-output.json.

Steps 6-9 (unchanged, imported/identical): first-time unblind line via log-append; frozen verdict_rules evaluated top-down with kot_common.eval_expr, missing/null metric => INCOMPLETE-DATA; PASS demoted to PASS-PENDING-AUDIT unless a CONFIRMED non-runner audit exists under registry/audits/truthstyle-2x2/ (verdict-gen.confirmed_audit); kot-verdict/1 object RT-14-linted and canonically written to registry/verdicts/truthstyle-2x2.json.

The emitted verdict object ADDITIONALLY carries inputs.analysis_invocation = {driver: 'tools/registry/verdict-gen-dts.py', driver_sha256: <sha256 of the committed driver bytes>, argv: ['--items','data/d-ts/items.jsonl','--labels','data/d-ts-labels/labels.jsonl'], corpus_digests_verified: {d-ts: <digest>, d-ts-labels: <digest>}} so the deviation from the generic spine is self-declaring to the auditor.

MOCK-GREEN GATE before the real readout: the coordinator must first green the driver end-to-end on a SCRATCH COPY of the repo root (synthetic labels from the pinned script's --mock path + a synthetic final row + a scratch seq-4 amendment), verifying it reproduces the expected mock verdict; the real registry/ and results-log/ are never touched by the mock.
post_run_order_of_operations: 1. three restarted judge passes complete (correction seq 1) -> runner finalize writes data/d-ts-labels/{labels.jsonl, judge-*-responses.jsonl, summary.json}
2. blinding-sweep.py GREEN over the completed run dir (correction seq 1) + RT-14 scrub gate; commit

3. ops amendment registry/amendments/truthstyle-2x2/4-pin-d-ts-labels.json (replace corpus_hashes.d-ts-labels placeholder with the kot-corpus-hash/1 digest, per the frozen 'AFTER adjudication completes and BEFORE the final-phase record' clause)

4. append the phase:final run record via log-append: event=run, phase=final, exit=ok, prereg_hash=frozen sha, config={seed:20260710}, artifacts=[labels.jsonl + summary.json + per-judge responses with sha256], metrics=RAW COUNTS ONLY (per-judge n_labelled/n_nolabel/transport retries/label counts - nothing in kot_common.FORBIDDEN_METRIC_KEYS)
5. run tools/registry/verdict-gen-dts.py (this ruling) -> analysis-output.json + unblind line + registry/verdicts/truthstyle-2x2.json

6. GATE-A ROUTING: verdict PASS => PASS-PENDING-AUDIT until the mandatory cross-vendor Codex GATE-A audit record lands CONFIRMED under registry/audits/truthstyle-2x2/ (P2 G-6; the auditor recomputes the verdict from the committed bytes, and MAY re-run the driver read-only). FAIL or INCONCLUSIVE => the frozen pre-declared conservative consumer rule holds: the style-leak guard on f2b-line LLM-judge endorsement readings STAYS IN FORCE and the measured interval is carried as a style-bias band (FAIL) or the record is never citable as reassurance (INCONCLUSIVE). Any fired instrument gate => INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never a hypothesis outcome. Interpretation of the readout is Fable's, never the runner's.
consumer_note: Equivalence claim, auditable: for this record the generic spine's stdin payload carries no information the pinned analysis consumes - the analysis reads exactly the two pinned corpora, which the driver digest-verifies against the (amended) frozen record at readout time; eligibility, completeness, unblind discipline, rule evaluation, audit gating, and RT-14 linting are the imported spine code paths, byte-identical in behaviour.
